payload.answer:
Required eval accounting fields typically include:

- token counts: Record prompt/input tokens, completion/output tokens, total tokens, and if available cached/read/write or reasoning tokens. These support reproducibility, cost estimation, and efficiency analysis.
- provider/model IDs: Record the LLM provider and exact model identifier/version used for each run. This is needed because behavior, pricing, latency, and tokenization vary by provider and model.
- estimated price: Calculate estimated cost from token counts and the provider/model pricing schedule in effect at run time. Include currency and, ideally, pricing version/date because prices can change.
- latency p50 p90 p95: Report percentile latencies across requests or eval cases. p50 is median latency, p90 is the latency below which 90% of requests completed, and p95 is the latency below which 95% completed. These summarize typical and tail performance better than an average alone.

entity_resolution.reason: The user asks for an explanation of evaluation accounting fields; no durable personal memory should be created.

details: Provided concise definitions for token accounting, model/provider identification, price estimation, and latency percentiles.
